questions.question: Explain the difference between a static nested class and a non-static inner class in Java with an example of when to use each.
keyPoints: Static nested class does not require outer class instance, cannot access non-static members directly
Non-static inner class requires outer class instance, has full access to all outer class members
Use static nested class when the nested class logically belongs with outer class but doesn't need outer class state
Use inner class when you need to access instance members of outer class or maintain tight coupling

questions.question: What are the four types of nested classes in Java? Explain each briefly.
keyPoints: Static nested class - defined with static keyword, no outer instance needed
Member inner class - non-static class declared at class level
Local inner class - defined inside a method or constructor
Anonymous inner class - unnamed class defined and instantiated in single expression

questions.question: Write Java code to create an instance of a non-static inner class from outside the outer class and explain the syntax.
keyPoints: First create outer class instance: OuterClass outer = new OuterClass();
Then create inner class instance: outer.new InnerClass();
Explain that .new is special syntax for inner class instantiation
The inner class instance is associated with the outer instance

questions.question: What is shadowing in the context of nested classes? How can you explicitly access a shadowed member of the outer class?
keyPoints: Shadowing occurs when nested class member has same name as outer class member
The nested class member takes precedence within nested class scope
Use OuterClassName.this.memberName to access outer class member
Example: Outer.this.x accesses x from Outer class when Inner also has x

questions.question: What are the restrictions on accessing variables from the enclosing scope in a local inner class?
keyPoints: Local inner class can only access local variables that are final or effectively final
Effectively final means variable is not modified after initialization
Cannot access non-final local variables
Can access instance and static members of outer class without restriction

questions.question: Write an example of an anonymous inner class that implements an interface.
keyPoints: Show interface definition
Demonstrate anonymous class syntax: new InterfaceName() { implementation }
Explain it creates and instantiates class in one expression
Show how it overrides interface methods

questions.question: What are the benefits of using nested classes in Java? Provide at least three advantages.
keyPoints: Logical grouping - classes only used in one place can be nested
Enhanced encapsulation - can access private members of outer class
Improved code readability - related classes are together
Better maintenance - logical organization makes code easier to understand
Can achieve cleaner API by making helper classes private

questions.question: Create a Java program demonstrating all four types of nested classes: static nested class, member inner class, local inner class, and anonymous inner class. Include proper instantiation and usage of each type.
keyPoints: Static nested class: declared with static, instantiated without outer instance
Member inner class: non-static at class level, needs outer instance
Local inner class: defined inside a method with its own methods
Anonymous inner class: implements Runnable interface with on-the-fly implementation
Proper access modifiers and demonstration of accessing outer class members
Compilation and execution showing each type works correctly

questions.question: Explain the concept of 'this' reference in nested classes. How does an inner class refer to its outer class instance? Write a Java program demonstrating this with a scenario where member names are shadowed.
keyPoints: Explain outerClassName.this syntax to reference outer class
Show shadowing scenario where both classes have same variable name
Demonstrate how to access outer class variable when shadowed
Write complete program with appropriate comments
Show how inner class can access all outer class members including private

questions.question: Design a real-world scenario where using nested classes (specifically inner classes) would be appropriate. Implement the solution demonstrating encapsulation benefits.
keyPoints: Design scenario: perhaps a university with departments (outer) and courses (inner), or a car with engine components
Show how inner class accesses private data of outer class
Demonstrate encapsulation - outer class controls access to inner classes
Show practical benefits like bundled related classes
Implement with appropriate access modifiers demonstrating encapsulation

questions.question: Compare and contrast local inner classes and anonymous inner classes in Java. When would you choose one over the other? Provide code examples.
keyPoints: Local inner class has a name, can be instantiated multiple times, can have constructors
Anonymous inner class has no name, created and instantiated in single expression, cannot have constructors
Use local inner class when you need multiple instances or complex class with multiple methods
Use anonymous inner class for simple implementations (like event handlers, Runnable)
Show code examples of both approaches for similar functionality
Discuss readability trade-offs
